steps.title: Identify the key reputation at stake
steps.content: The deciding clue is in the second note: Plot earned a reputation for being “the first person to have discovered dinosaur remains.” The 1990 discovery's significance lies in how it affects this specific reputation — by finding evidence that Indigenous peoples may have possessed dinosaur remains centuries earlier (around 1100 CE).

steps.title: Match the challenge to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B directly connects the 1990 discovery to Plot's reputation by showing the significance: the evidence suggests that Plot may not have been the first person to discover dinosaur remains. The other choices either fail to address his reputation (C), narrow the challenge to one specific species (A), or overstate the evidence as disproof rather than a possibility (D).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
